## Detection logic

Supplier-circular flow â€” A â†’ B â†’ C â†’ A directed money cycle of length â‰Ą 3 among company entities, detected via bounded-depth BFS over PAID_TO edges. Strength scales inversely with cycle length (shorter cycles are more suspicious).

## Likelihood-ratio reasoning

OECD-BO Â§4.4 â€” closed-loop fund cycles among entities sharing common control are a textbook money-laundering signal. Default prior 0.30 + weight 0.8 reflects strong signal-independence from the rest of category F. The architect treats a 3-node cycle as near-conclusive; longer cycles (5â€“6 nodes) admit more legitimate explanations (genuine multi-tier supply chains).

## Known false-positive traps

- **Legitimate inter-company supply chains** in vertically-integrated groups.
- **Multi-tier subcontracting** in construction where the same prime contractor periodically buys back smaller services from its own subs.
- **Reciprocal-trade relationships** between long-established firms in related industries.

## Production wiring

âś… Production-ready. The Stage-2 graph-metric scheduler runs `detectSupplierCycles` (packages/db-neo4j/src/gds/supplier-cycles.ts) nightly: bounded-depth BFS (MAX_CYCLE_LEN=6, MAX_FANOUT=200, visited-set termination) over PAID_TO edges between company nodes. Outputs persist as `metadata.supplierCycleLength` + `metadata.supplierCycleMembers` + `metadata.circularFlowDetected` on the cycle members. Pattern reads `supplierCycleLength`.
